findings.evidence: Lines 205-216 prove the full naturality theorem for arbitrary `x : SubgroupFiberOrbitQuotient H b`; lines 221-230 restate it for one representative and prove it by `rw [subgroupFiberOrbitQuotientEquivQuotientGroup_mapOfLE]`.
findings.fix: Remove this lemma, or replace it with the actual simplified representative statement using `Subgroup.quotientMapOfLE_apply_mk` and `subgroupFiberOrbitQuotientEquivQuotientGroup_apply_smul`.
findings.issue: `subgroupFiberOrbitQuotientEquivQuotientGroup_mapOfLE_apply_smul` is only `subgroupFiberOrbitQuotientEquivQuotientGroup_mapOfLE` specialized to `subgroupFiberOrbitClass H (φ • e)`, with the same hypotheses and no stronger representative conclusion.

findings.evidence: Lines 235-251 prove the same iff from `[MulAction.IsPretransitive ...] [IsCancelSMul ...]`; lines 256-263 add `[TopologicalSpace B] [PreconnectedSpace E]`, `IsCoveringMap p`, and `IsRegular p`, then only `letI` those instances before applying the generic lemma.
findings.fix: Drop the regular specialization and use `subgroupFiberOrbitClass_eq_iff_quotientGroup_mk_inv_eq` after installing `hreg.fiber_isPretransitive b` and `fiber_isCancelSMul hp` at call sites, or make the regular theorem state a genuinely regular-specific result.
findings.issue: `regularSubgroupFiberOrbitClass_eq_iff_quotientGroup_mk_inv_eq` duplicates the preceding equality criterion with stronger regular-cover assumptions.

summary: Two public lemmas are still special-case duplicates of nearby more general API rather than natural-level statements.
